p.em: Technical implementation spec for high-performance messaging

div.p: Accessing a field in FlatBuffers compiles to: asm mov eax, [rdi + 4] ; load vtable offset movzx eax, word [rdi+eax+10] ; load field offset from vtable test eax, eax ; check if field is present je .default ; branch to default if offset is 0 movsd xmm0, [rdi + eax] ; direct load from buffer ret

🟦 Data Block - raw bytes
🟧 Processing - instructions
🟩 Result - extracted value
🟥 Allocation - costly heap ops
🟪 Metadata - VTables/Headers

g.text: L1 Data Misses: ~25 | L2 Misses: ~10 | L3 Misses: ~3

ul.li: Linear Scan: Every byte must be read/validated.
Pointer Chasing: DOM nodes linked via pointers, causing random access.
Branch Mispredict: Variable field order and string lengths confuse CPU.

g.text: L1 Hits: 2-3 | L1 Misses: 1-2 | L2 Misses: 0-1

ul.li: Single Load: Only specific bytes for requested field are accessed.
Locality: VTable and data often reside in same 64B cache line.
Zero Alloc: No temporary objects = Zero pressure on GC/Heap.
